added_assumptions.label: thm:exact-real-exceptional-atlas:def:real-atlas-handle
added_assumptions.statement: def:real-atlas-handle — cited classical real-closed-field CAD and Tarski-Seidenberg interface: existence, section/sector structure, sign invariance, and semialgebraic projection; no effectivity or complexity claim.
added_assumptions.source: Bochnak-Coste-Roy, Real Algebraic Geometry, Sec. 2.3 and Thm. 2.2.1; Basu-Pollack-Roy, Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry, Ch. 5.

added_assumptions.label: thm:exact-real-exceptional-atlas:def:effective-rational-groebner-cad-interface
added_assumptions.statement: def:effective-rational-groebner-cad-interface — cited paper-agnostic exact rational Groebner/elimination and effective CAD/QE interface with finite symbolic trace, exact algebraic root/sign data, cellwise truth and retention, and universal natural constants a >= 1 and b fixed before all input families certifying (max 2 (sD))^(2^(a*N+b)).
added_assumptions.source: Cox-Little-O'Shea, Ideals, Varieties, and Algorithms, 4th ed., Chs. 2-3; Collins 1975; Basu-Pollack-Roy, Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry, Chs. 11 and 14; Basu 2017 survey, Sec. 2.1.2, Thm. 2.4 for the explicit CAD complexity form.

flags.f3_filler_directive: CLAUSE-36 EXACT-ATLAS CONSTRUCTION PASS. Preserve authoritative F2 clauses 1-36, all existing proof bodies, and the compiled AtlasClause32Execution plus AtlasClause32Construction helpers. The source-correct interface now includes exact prefix-stage signs, a joint forward/reverse coordinate relation, positive complexity domain, and a post-elimination dependent-pipeline degree envelope. Keep atlasSourceDegreeBound as the exact pre-elimination incidence receipt; instantiate the combined bound with atlasChargedDegreeBound, which covers the actual forward/reverse elimination outputs, dependent intersection job/output, and CAD input. Build the sole remaining paper-specific constructor from ValidComplexity, realAtlasHandleOutput, and effectiveRationalGroebnerCADOutput to an EffectiveRealAtlasOutput plus AtlasMachineExecution. Use the actual sequential forward/reverse/intersection pipeline and its consuming CAD result; retain the intersection basis in the recursive stage equality; use exact prefix artifacts, deduplicated projected-cell representative/source-cover links, separate forward/reverse existential labels, direct negative signs, the three distinct base families, common observable coordinate presentation, and exact charged combined bound. Work leaves-first in focused atlas helpers, then close exactRealExceptionalAtlas. Do not use effectiveRealAtlasOutputOfPresentation circularly, assume MatchedOrderAtlasConstruction, add a premise/gate/axiom/sorry, weaken a field, bound generated CAD projection degrees by the input D, rerun retrieval, reopen D0/F1, or edit any of the three completed headline proof clusters. If a field remains impossible, return its full Lean type and a compiled counterexample under corrected clauses 33-36.
flags.f2_scaffold_directive:
(1) ONE @node TAG PER NODE. When a single core node is rendered as MULTIPLE Lean decls (a primary def plus companion/auxiliary defs — e.g. a set plus its closure and preimage, or a base map plus its arrow/reverse variant), place the '-- @node: <id>' tag on EXACTLY ONE (the primary) decl. Every companion decl for the same node MUST be left UNTAGGED (no '-- @node:' line). Duplicate @node ids on sibling decls are treated as unlinked and break the graph refresh.

(2) def:real-atlas-handle IS A CITED EXTERNAL TOOL — ITS LEAN PROP IS THE CLASSICAL THEOREM, NOT OUR ATLAS. def:real-atlas-handle is a REGISTERED CITED node (gate.ts, gate_class=cited, source cite:bcr-bpr-cad = Bochnak-Coste-Roy, Real Algebraic Geometry, Sec. 2.3 + Thm 2.2.1; Basu-Pollack-Roy, Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry, Ch. 5 Thm 5.6/Def 5.1/Def 5.5/Notation 5.15/Thm 5.16). It is NO LONGER sourced to Collins 1975: Collins is an ALGORITHM paper whose statement of record carries effectivity/complexity content this Prop does not and must not encode. NEVER re-introduce a computability/effectivity/Decidable conjunct and NEVER restore the Collins citation. A `cited` node is a BORROWED classical fact owed a SOURCE-MATCH, never a build, so its Lean proposition must encode the THEOREM OF RECORD: universally quantified, self-contained, and mentioning NONE of this paper's objects. Keep its tagged decl EXACTLY

    def realAtlasHandleOutput : Prop := RealClosedFieldCADInterface

in Handles.lean — with NO `(m : ℕ)` binder.

(2a) THE CITED STATEMENT LIVES IN Helpers/CADInterface.lean and must encode ALL THREE parts of Collins' statement of record — a bare existence claim is NOT enough (an F4 reviewer rejected that as cited-mismatch): `RealClosedFieldCADInterface := AdaptedCADConstruction ∧ TarskiSeidenbergProjection` (the structure is `IsAdaptedCAD`, NOT `IsCollinsCAD` - those decls were RENAMED and must not be renamed back), where AdaptedCADConstruction quantifies over EVERY dimension r, EVERY finite family A of real polynomials in r variables, and EVERY variable order, and asserts an `IsCollinsCAD` decomposition carrying (i) PROJECTION — sign-invariance for `A ∪ generatedCADProjectionFamily order A` (coefficients + discriminants + principal subresultants, iterated down the order), (ii) BASE+EXTENSION — every cell is an `IsRecursivelyLiftedCADCell` section/sector cell of that generated family, and (iii) DECISION — every sign condition built from A is decided by the cell (each cell is contained in or disjoint from its solution set); plus TarskiSeidenbergProjection (the projection of a semialgebraic set is semialgebraic). Do NOT weaken any of the three back to plain existence.

(2b) THE GENERIC CAD MACHINERY NOW LIVES IN Helpers/CADInterface.lean, NOT Handles.lean. `cadAsUnivariate`, `cadCoefficients`, `cadDiscriminants`, `cadPrincipalSubresultants`, `cadProjectionStep`, `generatedCADProjectionFamily`, `cadEraseCoordinate`, `cadRealRootsAt`, `IsCADAlgebraicRoot`, `IsCADLastAlgebraicRoot`, `IsRecursivelyLiftedCADCell` are coordinate-index-GENERIC (`{σ : Type} [DecidableEq σ]`) and are declared ONCE, in Helpers/CADInterface.lean. NEVER re-declare them in Handles.lean — that is a duplicate-declaration BUILD ERROR. Handles.lean imports them and instantiates them at `AtlasIncidenceCoord m`, so the atlas uses the VERY SAME operators the cited statement quantifies over. Handles.lean keeps only its atlas-specific defs (AtlasIncidenceCoord, atlasIncidenceEval, DefinesAtlasIncidenceEquations, atlasIncidenceVariableBlock, IsAtlasIncidenceVariableOrder, IsGeneratedCADProjectionFamily, bandLimitedRealExceptionalLocus, IsSemialgebraicCumCell, atlasObservableEval, SignInvariantOn, CylindricalCumCells, AtlasAssignment, RealAtlasCADData, realAtlasCADStratification).

(2c) THE CITED PROP MUST NOT ASSERT OUR ATLAS. THIS SUPERSEDES EVERY `sync_back_f2*` NOTE ON THIS NODE IN plan.json (attempts 1-6): they pin the OLD, UNFAITHFUL form `realAtlasHandleOutput (m : ℕ) : Prop := realAtlasCADStratification m`, which FAILED the F4 source-match as `cited-mismatch`. NEVER restore it. `Nonempty (RealAtlasCADData m)` is OUR bespoke CONCLUSION (the atlas for OUR incidence system); asserting it under a Collins citation cites a theorem for content it does not contain - Collins supplies neither the elimination-ideal generators of barE_m (a COMPLEX Zariski closure) nor the Q_K <-> real-source-feasibility equivalence (the truncated Hamburger moment problem). Those two ARE the deferred open question oeq:real-exceptional-atlas. Keep `realAtlasCADStratification m := Nonempty (RealAtlasCADData m)` in Handles.lean as an UNASSERTED, UNTAGGED def naming the handle's OUTPUT SHAPE, and keep `RealAtlasCADData`, `realAtlasHandle`, `realAtlasHandleReverse` exactly as they are - they render the handle's CONSTRUCTION. The SPECIALIZATION is DERIVED, never assumed: Helpers/AtlasCADBridge.lean proves `atlasIncidenceCAD_of_cited`, the cited engine instantiated at this paper's incidence coordinates. Do NOT re-render the cited decl as a constructive CAD data type, do NOT re-add an `(m : ℕ)` binder to it, and do NOT strip the citation. The other CAD/QE-dependent defs (def:global-feasible-fiber-decision, def:direction-selector) stay as their existing MATCHED operational-spec forms: an existential feasibility Prop plus a NAMED external decision-interface predicate (I-3 CAD-ordering + I-1 apolar certificate) - not a constructive in-run CAD.

(3) KEEP THE CRITICAL-PATH STATEMENTS AT FULL STRENGTH — never regress to a tractable weaker form. def:forward-lvlingam-class / def:reverse-lvlingam-class are the existential CLASS OF LAWS: `∃ (Ω) (_ : MeasurableSpace Ω) (μ) (S) (X Y) (params), IsProbabilityMeasure μ ∧ IndependentSources ∧ FiniteCumulants ∧ SourceNonGaussian ∧ (∀ j, ∫ S j ∂μ = 0) ∧ AxisModel ∧ NonCollinear ∧ NonzeroEdge ∧ P = μ.map (X,Y)` — existential witnesses + source-centering + pushforward-law realization, NOT a fixed-parameter conjunction. thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery must retain ALL of: relatively Euclidean-OPEN real feasibility (a nonempty open subset meeting realFeasibleRegion, NOT a single feasible witness); empty opposite-arrow fiber on each locus; squarefree factorization recovery of the finite-slope set D (QD.roots = the axis+slopes multiset); QD.eval 0 ≠ 0 (fixed axis omitted); the common contraction-kernel identity ker = ⟨Q_D⟩ (the homogeneous-degree-(m+2) annihilator iff `∃ c, q = c • Q_D`); and the vertical-vs-horizontal fixed-axis clause. def:generic-full-fiber-compatibility must carry all four components (E_m, its Zariski closure barE_m, and the two generic-locus preimages H^right_m/H^left_m). def:separation-handle records the two quotient fibers + Jacobian ranks WITHOUT imposing a universal cross-fiber rank equality.

(4) DECISION-INTERFACE DEFS MUST NOT BE VACUOUS. A finite semialgebraic sign table (IsFiniteSemialgebraicFunction) reads only FINITELY many (r,a) coordinates, but CumVec = ℕ → ℕ → ℝ has infinitely many, and `Φ λ = t` constrains ALL of them (every cumulant map is 0 off the retained band). So an UNRESTRICTED `∀ t, decide t = true ↔ feasibleFiberFormula m Φ t` is UNSATISFIABLE — it silently collapses the decision Prop to False. Therefore: (a) every CAD/decision iff (IsOrderedFeasibleFiberCADDecision, ApolarFiberDecisionCertificate) MUST be guarded by `BandSupported m t` (t vanishes off `2 ≤ r ≤ 2m+2, a ≤ r`); (b) def:global-feasible-fiber-decision renders as a PAIR `feasibleFiberDecision (m) (b) (Φ) : (CumVec ℝ → Prop) × Prop := (fun t => feasibleFiberFormula m Φ t, feasibleFiberDecisionInterfaces m b Φ)` — the SAME shape as directionSelectorWithDecision. NEVER conjoin the t-INDEPENDENT operational decision structure into the per-t Prop: that makes FeasFiber^b_m(t) entail the global existence of the decision procedure and renders it identically False, instead of 'true exactly when R^b_{m,K}(t) ∩ F^b_{m,K} ≠ ∅'. Keep BOTH components (feasibility formula + operational interfaces), held separately.

(5) FINITE AMBIENT SPACES — THE PAPER'S SPACES ARE FINITE, THE LEAN TYPES ARE ℕ-INDEXED. ParamSpace's weight family and CumVec are ℕ-indexed, but the paper's ambients are the FINITE Θ^b_{m,L} = ℂ^{m+1} × ℂ^{n(L-1)} and ℂ^{q_L} (retained range 2 ≤ r ≤ L, a ≤ r). EVERY set the paper carves out of one of these MUST be cut down to it, or the ℕ-indexed representation silently turns it into a CYLINDER over unconstrained off-band coordinates. Keep EXACTLY these settled forms (all in Basic/Cumulants.lean unless noted):
  (a) `bandSupportedParams {R} [Zero R] (m L) : Set (ParamSpace R m)` — weights vanish for r < 2 ∨ L < r (complex mirror of the pinning clause realFeasibleRegion already imposes over ℝ).
  (b) `bandSupportedCumulants {R} [Zero R] (L) : Set (CumVec R)` — t r a = 0 off the retained range. Every truncation and arrow map lands here by construction.
  (c) `fiberCorrespondence {R} [Zero R] (L) (Φ) (t) := bandSupportedParams m L ∩ {θ | ∀ r a, 2 ≤ r → r ≤ L → a ≤ r → Φ θ r a = t r a}` — the fiber must BOTH pin θ to the band AND compare Φ θ against t on retained coordinates ONLY (comparing at every ℕ×ℕ coordinate lets off-band junk in t empty the fiber spuriously).
  (d) `genericFullFiberCompatibility` (E_m) must be intersected with `bandSupportedCumulants (2m+2)`; \bar E_m and the preimages H^b_m then inherit it.
  (e) any 'fiber is a single G_m-orbit' clause (oeq:generic-separation, oeq:minimal-real-information-order) must be guarded by `bandSupportedParams m (2m+2)` on BOTH the base point and the fiber.
  (f) any CAD/decision iff (Selector.lean IsOrderedFeasibleFiberCADDecision, ApolarFiberDecisionCertificate) must be guarded by band-support on t — see clause (4).
THESE ARE FAITHFUL BOOKKEEPING, NOT WEAKENINGS: on the t that actually arise (t = Φ^b(θ), which vanishes off the band by construction) each agrees with the naive form. In particular thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery's empty-opposite-fiber conjuncts are UNCHANGED in meaning and must stay UNGUARDED. NEVER pin the band inside genericParameterLocus or Ur/Ul — the band-support slice is a proper subspace and that would destroy the required Zariski density.

(5) NO GATE ON THE FLAGSHIP — IT IS UNCONDITIONAL. `ass:truncated-moment-interior` has been DISCHARGED: `TruncatedMomentInterior L` is now PROVEN in-run (`theorem truncatedMomentInterior (L : ℕ) : TruncatedMomentInterior L`, Helpers/MomentGateInterior.lean, 0 sorry, axiom-clean) and the gate registration has been retired via `bin/gate.ts --discharge`. `generic_apolar_arrow_recovery` therefore takes ONLY `(m : ℕ) (hm : ValidComplexity m)`. NEVER re-emit an `hgate : TruncatedMomentInterior …` binder (or any `_of_gate` hypothesis) on it: the premise is discharged internally by `truncatedMomentInterior (2*m+2)`. Re-adding it would WEAKEN a proven unconditional theorem.

(6) BAND-SUPPORT, TWO REMAINING SITES (both already patched in place — preserve them):
  (g) `genericCompatibilityPreimage L Φ := bandSupportedParams m L ∩ genericParameterLocus m L ∩ Φ ⁻¹' (genericFullFiberCompatibility m)`. The band conjunct is LOAD-BEARING and is NOT inherited from E_m: every `Φ^b` is 0 off the retained band by construction, so `(Φ^b)⁻¹(E_m)` places NO constraint on the off-band source weights `c_{j,r}` (r ∉ [2,L]) — without it H^b_m is a cylinder. The matching `H^b_m` characterization in TExceptionalLocus.lean must carry the SAME `θ ∈ bandSupportedParams m (2*m+2) ∧ …` conjunct on its RHS, or the iff is internally inconsistent.
  (h) `IsSemialgebraicCumCell (m) (cell)` (Handles.lean) must intersect with `bandSupportedCumulants (2*m+2)`, exactly as the parallel `IsSemialgebraicCumVec` does. Without it a cell is a cylinder over all but finitely many (r,a) coordinates, no finite union of such cells can equal `bandLimitedRealExceptionalLocus m` (which pins infinitely many off-band coordinates to 0), `RealAtlasCADData` becomes UNINHABITABLE and `realAtlasHandleOutput` collapses to a vacuous `False` the cited Collins CAD engine could never inhabit.

(7) ARROW MAPS ARE PINNED BY THE ARROW TAG. `feasibleFiberDecision (m) (b : Direction)` takes NO free `Φ` parameter: the note DETERMINES `Φ^b` from `b` (`directionCumulantMap m b`). Never re-expose an independent `Φ` argument on a decl that already carries the arrow tag `b` — it would let the decision be read off an arrow map unrelated to the fixed axis the apolar certificate consults.

(5) ZARISKI OPENNESS/DENSITY IS BAND-RELATIVE — NEVER RE-RENDER IT IN THE FULL AMBIENT. The paper's structural parameter space is the FINITE ambient Theta^b_{m,L} = C^{m+1} x C^{n(L-1)}; Lean's ParamSpace is N-indexed, so that ambient is represented by the subset `bandSupportedParams m L`. Every Zariski openness/density claim about a parameter locus MUST therefore use the RELATIVE predicates `IsZariskiOpenParamIn L U` / `IsZariskiDenseParamIn L U` (relative to `bandSupportedParams m L`), NOT the full-ambient `IsZariskiOpenParam` / `IsZariskiDenseParam`. Concretely: in thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery, oeq:generic-separation and oeq:minimal-real-information-order the open/dense conjuncts are `IsZariskiOpenParamIn (2*m+2) Ur` etc., and the recovery loci are `bandSupportedParams m (2*m+2) INTERSECT {theta | <nonvanishing polynomial> != 0}`. Rendering these in the full N-indexed ambient is UNFAITHFUL and makes the conjunction `Ur SUBSET genericParameterLocus AND IsZariskiDenseParam Ur` unsatisfiable (band-support is a proper Zariski-CLOSED subset there, so a full-ambient Zariski-open subset of it is empty). Likewise KEEP `genericParameterLocus m L = bandSupportedParams m L INTERSECT {nonvanishing generic product}` as a two-factor intersection; do NOT drop its band factor.

(8) THE APOLAR CERTIFICATE MUST CHARACTERISE Q_D BY THE CONTRACTION KERNEL (Selector.lean, def:global-feasible-fiber-decision + def:direction-selector). An F4 reviewer correctly flagged `derived` (paper implies Lean, not conversely) because ApolarFiberDecisionCertificate recorded only an abstract recovered support + product annihilator, omitting the note's DEFINING characterisation of Q_D: the note says Q_D is factored "from the contractions of the divided-power forms f_{n+k}" (core def:global-feasible-fiber-decision; writeup.tex thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery: "the common kernel of q |-> (q(partial) f_{n+k})_{0<=k<=n-2} is the line generated by the squarefree degree-n support annihilator Q_D"). ApolarFiberDecisionCertificate now carries TWO ADDITIONAL CONJUNCTS and they are LOAD-BEARING - never drop them, never weaken them:
  (a) an evaluation link `annihilator t z = MvPolynomial.eval ![z.1, z.2] (realSupportAnnihilatorPoly (support t))`; and
  (b) the KERNEL IDENTITY: for every t in rankOpen and every homogeneous degree-(m+2) q, `(forall k <= m, realDiffApply q (realDividedPowerBlock t (m+2+k)) = 0) <-> exists c, q = c . realSupportAnnihilatorPoly (support t)`.
The three supporting defs `realDividedPowerBlock`, `realDiffApply`, `realSupportAnnihilatorPoly` are declared ONCE, in Selector.lean (real mirrors of the complex `dividedPowerBlock`/`diffApply`/`supportAnnihilator` in Helpers/ApolarDefs.lean). Do NOT re-declare them elsewhere (duplicate-declaration BUILD ERROR) and do NOT re-render the certificate back to the support-only form.

(9) NO PROOF-METHOD CONJUNCTS IN A DEF (def:real-twin-construction-handle, Handles.lean). `compactlySupportedFeasibleRegion` renders the note's "compactly supported non-Gaussian laws" and NOTHING MORE. The note's phrase "through a truncated-moment-matrix perturbation" is the note's METHOD OF EXHIBITING those laws, not a property OF a law - "was obtained by a perturbation" is not a predicate on a measure. NEVER add a perturbation / positive-density / moment-interior conjunct to `compactlySupportedFeasibleRegion` or to `realTwinConstructionHandle`: that would make the Lean predicate STRICTLY STRONGER than the object the note defines (drift). The perturbation content the run uses is a PROVED theorem (`truncatedMomentInterior`, Helpers/MomentGateInterior.lean), not an assumed clause. The docstring says so explicitly - keep it.

(10) THE CITED CAD CELL LANGUAGE MUST STAY SATISFIABLE (Helpers/CADInterface.lean). An F4 reviewer found the cited Prop was UNSATISFIABLE - i.e. we were assuming FALSE, which would make every consumer of the citation vacuously true. Two fixes are now in place and are LOAD-BEARING; NEVER revert either:
  (a) `generatedCADProjectionFamily order family = order.foldl (fun fam x => fam UNION cadProjectionStep x fam) family` - it ACCUMULATES every projection stage (BPR Sec. 11.1 cylindrifying family C(A) = union_i C_i(A)). The old replacing fold `(fun fam x => cadProjectionStep x fam)` returned ONLY the last stage - the family with every variable already eliminated, i.e. CONSTANTS - so lifting against it found no roots and no cell could exist.
  (b) `IsRecursivelyLiftedCADCell` takes the family as a RECURSION-VARYING first argument and projects it (`cadProjectionStep x family`) on each descent (BPR's stage-specific C_i), and it has FIVE cell constructors, the first being the ell=0 case: `((forall a in base, cadRealRootsAt x family a = EMPTY) AND cell = {a | cadEraseCoordinate x a IN base})` - when the stage family has no real root over the base, the cited theorem puts the WHOLE FIBRE above it. Omitting that case is what made the statement false at r=1, A=EMPTY.
  `IsAdaptedCAD.recursively_lifted` therefore reads `forall i, IsRecursivelyLiftedCADCell A order (cell i)` - starting from the INPUT family A, NOT from `generatedCADProjectionFamily order A`. `RealAtlasCADData.forward_recursive`/`reverse_recursive` likewise start from the INCIDENCE union, not from `projectionFamily`.
  The non-vacuity receipt `isRecursivelyLiftedCADCell_univ_of_empty` (CADInterface.lean, PROVEN, 0 sorry) discharges exactly the reviewer's counterexample and must be kept.

(11) CYLINDRICITY IS ORDER-RELATIVE (Helpers/CADInterface.lean). A second F4 reviewer found a second falsity in the cited Prop: `AdaptedCADConstruction` quantifies over EVERY Nodup variable `order`, but `IsAdaptedCAD.cylindrical` fixed cylindricity to the NATURAL coordinate-index prefixes (`cadTruncate k`, keeping i < k), so lifting order and cylindricity order disagreed for all but one order - and even there they named OPPOSITE coordinate sets, because IsRecursivelyLiftedCADCell peels the HEAD of the order first and zeroes it, so the live base at depth k is `order.drop k` (a SUFFIX), not an index prefix. FIX (in place, keep it): `cadTruncate` is DELETED; `cadOrderTruncate (live : List sigma) a = fun i => if i IN live then a i else 0`; `IsCylindricallyArranged (order) (cell)` quantifies over depth k and compares images under `cadOrderTruncate (order.drop k)`; `IsAdaptedCAD.cylindrical : IsCylindricallyArranged order cell`. NEVER reintroduce an index-prefix truncation.

(12) KEEP BOTH NON-VACUITY RECEIPTS (Helpers/CADInterface.lean, both PROVEN, 0 sorry). `isRecursivelyLiftedCADCell_univ_of_empty` and `isAdaptedCAD_univ_of_empty` exhibit the trivial instance (r=1, A=empty, the single cell R) satisfying the recursive cell language and EVERY field of the IsAdaptedCAD record simultaneously. They are the anti-vacuity guard for an ASSUMED Prop: without them a defective encoding makes RealClosedFieldCADInterface FALSE and every consumer of the citation vacuously true. Any future edit to IsAdaptedCAD / IsRecursivelyLiftedCADCell / IsCylindricallyArranged MUST keep these two theorems compiling.

MANUAL RESOLVED-OEQ SALVAGE (authoritative for this F2 rewind):
- oeq:generic-separation was removed and replaced by proved thm:generic-arrow-recovery-and-fiber-obstruction; emit a theorem named genericArrowRecoveryAndFiberObstruction from the answer statement and proof in discovery/core.json.
- oeq:generic-exceptional-locus was removed and replaced by proved thm:exceptional-locus-codimension-one; emit a theorem named exceptionalLocusCodimensionOne from the answer statement and proof in discovery/core.json.
- oeq:minimal-real-information-order was removed and replaced by proved thm:improved-real-information-order; emit a theorem named improvedRealInformationOrder from the answer statement and proof in discovery/core.json.
- oeq:real-exceptional-atlas was removed and replaced by proved thm:exact-real-exceptional-atlas; emit a theorem named exactRealExceptionalAtlas from the answer statement and proof in discovery/core.json.
Any legacy formalization.md prose that still presents one of those nodes as an open question is superseded by core.json and plan.json. Do not recreate an oeq: Prop def.


(13) EXACT REAL ATLAS THEOREM — EFFECTIVE PAPER-SPECIFIC OUTPUT AND m >= 1. F2.5 found genuine plan/Lean drift against the corrected core. Keep realAtlasHandleOutput EXACTLY the classical paper-agnostic RealClosedFieldCADInterface required by clauses (2)-(2c); NEVER add effectivity to that cited interface. Instead add a paper-specific effective-atlas output predicate/structure that packages a RealAtlasCADData m with explicit forward and reverse Boolean evaluators, each realized by IsFiniteSemialgebraicFunction (a finite observable-polynomial sign table), and proves on every band-limited exceptional t that its true value is equivalent to nonemptiness of the corresponding complete real feasible fiber / equivalently to the atlas cell label. The theorem exactRealExceptionalAtlas must take (m : Nat) (hm : ValidComplexity m) and hCAD : realAtlasHandleOutput, and conclude nonemptiness of this EFFECTIVE/evaluable atlas output. Bare Nonempty (RealAtlasCADData m) is too weak and is forbidden. This paper-specific evaluator certificate is DERIVED, never assumed, and must preserve full fibers plus singular/nongeneric/boundary branches.

(14) EXACT REAL ATLAS AFTER WITNESS ELIMINATION — LAMBDA CELLS PLUS A FINITE SYMBOLIC PROGRAM. This clause refines clause (13) after the second F2.5 source audit. The displayed source-incidence degree formula and the degree parameter charged to the combined elimination/CAD complexity theorem are distinct. In the paper-agnostic dependent interface, choose the positive envelope D after the concrete forward/reverse elimination pipeline has been returned and require it to bound both supplied source jobs, their actual saturated elimination bases, the dependent intersection job built from those outputs, the actual intersection basis, and the dependent CAD input. This is a conditional finite envelope; never claim that elimination preserves the original input degree and never require internally generated CAD projection-family degrees to remain <= D, since their growth is accounted for by the cited doubly-exponential algorithmic bound. In the paper specialization, keep atlasSourceDegreeBound as the exact pre-elimination incidence receipt and define a separate chargedDegreeBound as a finite maximum over that source bound and the actual cited elimination/intersection/CAD inputs and outputs. Prove sourceDegreeBound <= chargedDegreeBound and 1 <= chargedDegreeBound, then use chargedDegreeBound in the exact combined bound. Preserve the clause-35 joint coordinate relation, dependent quantifier order, exact charged trace, three-family atlas transport, deduplicated retention, complete forward/reverse fibers, all singular/nongeneric/boundary branches, and every other existing constraint. This is a narrow source-interface correction required by the authoritative D0 statement that D_m is the maximum degree after finite elimination; it is not a theorem weakening, new paper-specific premise, gate, discovery rewind, or permission to reopen the three completed headline theorems. F2 revise only; F2.5 reviews the effective cited node and exact-atlas degree closure delta.

(37) UNDELIVERED SECONDARY ATLAS OVERRIDE. The plan now authoritatively classifies thm:exact-real-exceptional-atlas as delivery_status=undelivered, role=secondary, after citation-instantiation-overflow. This supersedes clauses (1)-(36) only insofar as they require emitting or proving that atlas theorem. On every F2 revise, remove/keep absent its Lean declaration and @node tag; do not recreate it as a theorem, Prop definition, axiom, gate, weakened claim, or sorry. Preserve every completed delivered theorem and every non-atlas constraint above. The typed atlas statement and reason remain in core/plan/graph for presentation as a remark.

source_rewind.review_scope: Only the effective cited CAD node and exact-real-atlas post-elimination charged-degree closure; do not reopen the three completed headline theorems.

flags.f1_revise_directive: Re-plan the flagged nodes in formalization.md: (1) Split oeq:real-exceptional-atlas's single bundled `lean_kind:"assumption"` gate into TWO separate named external interfaces — I-3 (generic real-QE/CAD decision correctness) and I-4 (truncated-Hamburger Hankel-PSD atomic representation) — and re-plan the atlas as a conditional DERIVED theorem over them, keeping the project-specific feasibility/incidence/elimination reductions as local derivations (not fused into the gate). (2) Thread I-1 (apolarity/catalecticant) and I-2 (Zariski/real-open dimension) as EXPLICIT external assumption interfaces in the `hyps` of every consuming deferred decl (thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery, oeq:generic-separation, oeq:generic-exceptional-locus, oeq:minimal-real-information-order), and set defer_tier/external-conditional honestly rather than build-inline. (3) Replace the Prop-only feasibility/selector defs (def:global-feasible-fiber-decision, def:direction-selector, def:real-atlas-handle) with the QE-interface-threaded conditional-evaluability form so the finite semialgebraic decision is gated on I-3, not silently claimed. (4) Update S1's substrate survey to survey-and-reject Causalean.Discovery.LiNGAM.Kurtosis.cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum with its concrete abstraction mismatch (two-moment/finite-order vs overcomplete all-order LvLiNGAM), recording it as bypass-justified. Change no .tex math.

proposed_from.topic: Chen et al.'s (arXiv:2510.22711, 2025) order-(2m+3) higher-cumulant rank-deficiency test for the causal DIRECTION between two variables X,Y under m non-collinear latent confounders is NOT minimal: the direction is GENERICALLY identifiable from the joint cumulant tensor TRUNCATED at order 2m+2 (one order lower). Kernel: reading the stacked truncated cumulant tensor T_{2m+2}(X,Y) as two axis-conditioned graded cumulant-image varieties (a simultaneous binary-form / Sylvester decomposition), prove (I) [strict lower-order sufficiency] the two opposite-arrow cumulant-image varieties GENERICALLY SEPARATE at K=2m+2, so the arrow is recovered off a proper subvariety via the simultaneous-binary-form recovery map — strictly improving the anchor's order-2m+3 requirement (minimal order K*(m)=2m+2, pinned by the DOF balance p=nK-1 vs q with n=m+2); and (II) [exact exceptional non-ID locus] the two varieties INTERSECT in an explicit CODIMENSION-m exceptional variety E_m (the axis-conditioned Waring-alternative compatibility system) on which an opposite-arrow twin shares T_{2m+2}, so identification fails exactly on E_m — give E_m's defining equations and prove codim E_m = m. Deliver worked m=1 (order-4 truncation, E_1 codim-1) and m=2 (order-6, E_2 codim-2) instances then the general dichotomy. Motif M18 (structure identification: recover the arrow up to the direction indeterminacy via cumulant asymmetry; recovery map + exceptional locus are the indeterminacy characterization). ExactID structure-ID; distinct from coefficient-identification (pins the minimal cumulant ORDER and the exceptional locus of ARROW identifiability, not a coefficient's value).
